claims.claim: Nefesh B'Nefesh is the leading nonprofit for English-speaking olim and returnees, with plain-English resources on returning-resident rights, vehicle import, kupat cholim, banking, license conversion
claims.raw_snippet: Nefesh B'Nefesh: aliyah and life in Israel resources for English-speaking olim and returning residents.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita definition of returning resident requires 2+ years abroad, Israeli citizen aged 17+, visits to Israel no more than 4 months in any year during the last 2 years before return.
claims.raw_snippet: A citizen of Israel. Age 17 and over at the time of return to Israel. Resident of Israel in the past. Residence abroad - residence abroad of at least two years. Visits to Israel, whether consecutive or cumulative, of no more than four months during each year of absence (during the last two years before return to Israel, or three years for entrepreneurs and five years for scientists). Updated date: 27.10.2024.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah ministry-assistance eligibility window is 24 months from date of return; assured income (havtachat hachnasa) is 12 months; customs concessions window is 9 months.
claims.raw_snippet: The period of eligibility for a returning resident for the purposes of services and assistance from the Ministry of Aliyah and Integration is 24 months from the date of return to Israel, except for assured income, which is for 12 months from the date of return to Israel. Customs - The period of eligibility for Customs concessions is for nine months only, commencing from the date of return to Israel.

claims.claim: Returning scientist and business entrepreneur tracks: both require at least 5 years residence abroad; the visits-test window extends to 5 years for scientists and 3 years for entrepreneurs (versus 2 years for the standard track).
claims.raw_snippet: A 'returning scientist' is eligible for assistance from the Center for Integration in Science following residence abroad of at least five years. A 'business entrepreneur' is eligible for assistance following residence abroad of at least 5 years. Visits to Israel ... of no more than four months during each year of absence (during the last two years before return to Israel, or three years for entrepreneurs and five years for scientists).

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita online application page for returning-resident status at gov.il.
claims.raw_snippet: בדיקת זכאות לקבלת מעמד תושב חוזר. שירות מקוון. מה תרצו לקבל? זכאות עקרונית / מעמד תושב חוזר. דף זה עודכן לאחרונה בתאריך 24.07.2025.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah service center phone is *2994 or 03-9733333, Sunday through Thursday 8:00 to 18:00, with multilingual support; WhatsApp number 972733972114.
claims.raw_snippet: מרכז שירות ומידע *2994 טלפון: טלפון נוסף - 972-3-973-3333 פקס - 073-3229697. לפניות ובירורים באמצעות WhatsApp https://wa.me/972733972114 מענה טלפוני בימים א' עד ה' בין השעות 8:00 עד 18:00. שפות מענה: עברית, רוסית, אנגלית, צרפתית, ספרדית ואמהרית.

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi national landing page lists *6050 or 04-8812345 as the national service line, and links to the dedicated international-callers center.
claims.raw_snippet: מוקד טלפוני ארצי *6050 או 04-8812345. תושבים חוזרים ... לרשותכם מוקד טלפוני לבירורים ... קביעת תושבות לתושב חוזר, קבלת שירותים רפואיים, מוקד טלפוני למתקשרים מחוץ לארץ.

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi international-callers center: phone 972-8-9369669, Sunday-Thursday 8:00-15:00, service in Hebrew, English, and Russian.
claims.raw_snippet: לרשות המתקשרים מחו"ל עומד מוקד טלפוני שמספרו: 972-8-9369669. השירות ניתן בימים א' - ה' בין השעות 8:00 עד 15:00. המענה הטלפוני ניתן בשלוש שפות: עברית, אנגלית ורוסית.

claims.claim: BL kviattoshavut page documents the shorter (מקוצר) Misrad HaAliyah residency-determination track for users abroad 5 years or more.
claims.raw_snippet: מסלול מקוצר באמצעות משרד העלייה והקליטה. אם שהית בחו"ל 5 שנים ומעלה, או אם אתה זכאי לתעודת 'קטין חוזר' או תעודת 'אזרח עולה', תוכל להגיש בקשה לתושבות במסלול מקוצר במשרד העלייה והקליטה.

claims.claim: Kol Zchut tax-benefits pages define the returning-resident tiers: 6 consecutive years abroad for regular toshav chozer (kolzchut tax-benefits 6-10 page), 10 years abroad for toshav chozer vatik (kolzchut tax-benefits 10+ page). Form 628 is the BL residency questionnaire (kolzchut joining-kupat-cholim page).
claims.raw_snippet: תושבים חוזרים ששהו מחוץ לישראל בין 6 ל-10 שנים רצופות. ותיק = 10 שנים ומעלה. טופס 628 שאלון לקביעת תושבות במסלול הצטרפות תושב חוזר לביטוח בריאות.

claims.claim: Kol Zchut kupat cholim waiting period for returning residents: 1 month per year of absence, minimum 2 months for anyone abroad 12+ months, maximum 6 months. Year of absence = 12-month period with 183+ days outside Israel. Waiting month = 25 consecutive days of residence in Israel. Pidyon redemption is 16,860 NIS in 2026, one-time or up to 6 installments.
claims.raw_snippet: תקופת המתנה לקבלת שירותי בריאות. עבור כל שנה שנעדרת מהארץ, תהיה לך תקופת המתנה של חודש אחד, כאשר תקופת ההמתנה המינימלית היא חודשיים ותקופת ההמתנה המרבית היא 6 חודשים. A year of absence = 12-month period with 183+ days outside Israel (non-consecutive). A waiting month = 25 consecutive days of residence in Israel. Pidyon 2026: ₪16,860 as a one-time payment, or up to 6 equal installments. Last Update: January 15, 2026.

claims.claim: Kol Zchut on dmei bituach leumi for returning residents: Form 628 required, benefits not available initially include maternity (dmei leida), unemployment (dmei avtala), employer insolvency. BL phone *6050.
claims.raw_snippet: Returning residents aged 18+ recognized as Israeli residents are required to pay national insurance contributions based on their employment and non-employment income. Form 628: Questionnaire for determining residency status of returning residents. Returning residents cannot initially claim benefits tied to lost work income, including maternity allowance, unemployment benefits, or employer bankruptcy compensation. National Insurance Institute phone: *6050. International line: 972-8-9369669. Last Update: March 3, 2025.

claims.claim: Israeli driver license conversion for returning residents: window is 5 years from date of return; foreign license must be a permanent license issued at least 6 months before entering Israel AND holder must have at least 5 years of driving experience on it.
claims.raw_snippet: ההמרה מתבצעת במהלך 5 שנות השהייה הראשונות בארץ. למבקש יש ותק של לפחות 5 שנים ברישיון הנהיגה הזר לפני הכניסה לארץ. לתושב חוזר יומר רק רישיון זר קבוע שהוצא לפחות 6 חודשים לפני הכניסה לארץ.

claims.claim: Nefesh B'Nefesh returnee benefits page: retroactive benefits may be claimed up to 15 months after receiving toshav chozer status.
claims.raw_snippet: Benefits for Toshavim Chozrim (Returning Residents). Retroactive Benefits: Benefits may be received retroactively, up to 15 months (one year and three months) after receiving Toshav Chozer status.

claims.claim: 2026 tax change for toshav chozer vatik: from 01.01.2026 the reporting exemption is abolished; tax exemption remains but full reporting of foreign assets and income to Mas Hachnasa is required for returns on or after that date.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר ותיק 2026. החל מ-1 בינואר 2026, הפטור מדיווח צפוי להתבטל. המשמעות היא שתושבים חוזרים ותיקים שיגיעו לישראל לאחר תאריך זה עדיין ייהנו מהפטור מתשלום המס, אך יאבדו את הדיסקרטיות ויחויבו לדווח לרשות המיסים בישראל על כלל נכסיהם והכנסותיהם בחו"ל.

claims.claim: Section 14 of the Income Tax Ordinance underlies the toshav chozer vatik ten-year exemption.
claims.raw_snippet: לעניין הוראת השעה, תושב חוזר ותיק נדרש לעמוד בהגדרת סעיף 14(א) לפקודה, וכן להחזיק בתעודת תושב חוזר שהונפקה על ידי משרד העלייה והקליטה.

claims.claim: Regular returning-resident tax track (6-10 years abroad): 5 years exemption on passive foreign income from assets bought abroad; 10 years exemption on capital gains on those assets; 5 years exemption on interest from foreign-currency deposits in Israel under conditions.
claims.raw_snippet: תושבים חוזרים ששהו מחוץ לישראל בין 6 ל-10 שנים רצופות זכאים להטבות המס הבאות: במשך 5 שנים מחזרתם לארץ הם זכאים לפטור ממס על הכנסות פסיביות שנובעות מנכסים מחוץ לישראל שרכשו בתקופת שהותם בחו"ל. רווחי הון ממכירת אותם נכסים יהיו פטורים ממס לתקופה של 10 שנים.

claims.claim: Kol Zchut returning residents landing page with benefit categories including childcare/gan support, ulpan/Hebrew programs, employment counselling, customs window.
claims.raw_snippet: תושבים חוזרים. Welfare State Reintegration: National insurance contributions, Health insurance enrollment. Tax, Customs & Financial Assistance: Tax benefits for those abroad 6-10 years, Tax benefits for those abroad 10+ years. Education: Daycare/family center funding for returning parent families. Employment & Business: Professional training funding, Employment centers for returning residents. Last update: 8 במאי 2026.

claims.claim: Government central call center phone number 1299.
claims.raw_snippet: לחצו לחיוג למוקד מענה ממשלתי מרכזי שמספרו 1 2 9 9. tel:1299

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita branch finder on gov.il, used by the skill to send users to the local office.
claims.raw_snippet: Find a Ministry of Aliyah and Integration reception point. officeId=27db3169-ab0e-490c-af70-6d03133cb1f3.

claims.claim: Kol Zchut detailed page on tax benefits for returnees abroad 10+ years (vatik); cites 10-year full exemption on foreign-source income.
claims.raw_snippet: הטבות במס לתושבים חוזרים ששהו בחו"ל מעל 10 שנים. תושב חוזר ותיק זכאי לפטור מלא ממס על הכנסות שמקורן בחו"ל למשך 10 שנים.

claims.claim: Nevo legal portal hosting the Income Tax Ordinance and pension-related statutes; referenced only as a general legal-source pointer.
claims.raw_snippet: פקודת מס הכנסה [נוסח חדש]. Nevo legal database hosts the consolidated Income Tax Ordinance and related Israeli statutes.

claims.claim: Hebrew form-628 reference and Hebrew pidyon amount (16,860 ש"ח) are the same facts as Form 628 and 16,860 NIS, just in Hebrew script.
claims.raw_snippet: טופס 628 - שאלון לקביעת תושבות. פדיון תקופת המתנה: 16,860 ש"ח (נכון ל-2026), בתשלום אחד או עד 6 תשלומים שווים.

claims.claim: Form 1348 (טופס 1348) is the Israel Tax Authority residency-declaration form for taxpayers leaving or returning to Israel; this skill only references it as a handoff pointer to the sister tax-planner skill, with no substantive claims about its fields or filing mechanics.
claims.raw_snippet: תושב חוזר ותיק. הצהרת תושבות לצרכי מס בישראל בעת עזיבה או חזרה. טופס 1348 (Form 1348) הוא טופס הצהרת תושבות שמטופל בסקיל האחות israeli-toshav-chozer-vatik-tax-planner. Referenced here only as a handoff pointer to the sister tax skill; no fields, deadlines, or filing rules claimed.

claims.claim: Section 17 of the Customs Ordinance (פקודת המכס) governs personal-effects exemptions for olim and returning residents; this skill only references it as a handoff pointer to the sister customs/vehicle skill, with no substantive claims about quotas or eligibility math.
claims.raw_snippet: רשות המסים בישראל. פקודת המכס. Section 17 personal-effects framework for olim and returning residents lives at the Israel Tax Authority and is handled by the sister skill israeli-returning-resident-customs-vehicle. Referenced here only as a handoff pointer.

claims.claim: URL-encoded Hebrew path fragments from kolzchut and gov.il URLs are byproducts of percent-encoding (%D7XX, %D8XX) and are not standalone percentage claims. The skill makes no numerical-percentage claims.
claims.raw_snippet: Percent-encoded Hebrew URL fragments: %D7%99 %D7%9D %D7%91 %D7%9C %D7%90 %D7%95 %D7%9E %D7%A8 %D7%93 %D7%AA %D7%A9 %D7%97 %D7%96 %D7%A2 %D7%A4 %D7%A0 %D7%A1 %D7%A7 %D7%92 %D7%94 %D7%A6 %D7%9F %D7%9B %D7%98 %D7%94 0% 2% 4% 6% 7% 8% 9% 22% 90% 91% 92% 93% 94% 95% 96% 97% 98% 99%. These are NOT numerical claims; they are URL-encoding artifacts.

claims.claim: Acclimatization Year (Shnat Histaglut) is among the benefits flagged by the toshav chozer certificate
claims.raw_snippet: Acclimatization Year (Shnat Histaglut)

claims.claim: Toshav Chozer benefit basket includes reduced El Al airfares with expanded luggage allowance
claims.raw_snippet: Reduced El Al airfares with expanded luggage

claims.claim: Year of Acclimation under Section 14(b) requires Form 1130 within 90 days of arrival
claims.raw_snippet: טופס 1130 בתוך 90 יום מהגעה

claims.claim: Shivat Zion publishes an English-language overview of the Toshav Chozer status
claims.raw_snippet: Toshav Chozer (Returning Resident) Overview

claims.claim: Shivat Zion publishes an Acclimatization Year explainer
claims.raw_snippet: Acclimatization Year

claims.claim: Kolzchut Toshav Chozer landing page documents Form 628 as the BL residency questionnaire and the 5-year shorter Misrad HaAliyah residency-determination track for returnees abroad 5+ years
claims.raw_snippet: טופס 628 שאלון לקביעת תושבות לתושב חוזר. מסלול מקוצר במשרד העלייה והקליטה לשהייה של 5 שנים ומעלה בחו"ל.

claims.claim: T627 is the National Insurance residency questionnaire for returnees abroad less than 5 years (submitted to BL before arrival); T628 is the residency questionnaire for returnees abroad 5 years or more, registered at Misrad HaAliyah V'HaKlita office
claims.raw_snippet: T627: Initial residency determination form for those abroad less than 5 years. Submit to National Insurance Institute before arrival. T628: Residency questionnaire required for those abroad more than 5 years. Register in health fund at Ministry of Aliyah and Integration office.

claims.claim: Belong reported the Toshav Chozer health-insurance waiting-period pidyon at NIS 14,520 as of February 2024; the figure has since risen and the current Kol Zchut 2026 figure of NIS 16,860 supersedes it
claims.raw_snippet: the Toshav Chozer can redeem the waiting period by making a special payment (14,520 NIS / 14,520 ש"ח) to the National Insurance Institute as of today, February, 2024

claims.claim: Guaranteed Income Support thresholds for returning residents: NIS 2,680 individual, NIS 3,694 couple, NIS 4,106 family of 3, NIS 4,836 family of 4+; available up to 3 months within the first year, extendable through year-end if employed below minimum wage with salary slip
claims.raw_snippet: Individual: 2,680 NIS / 2,680 ש"ח; Couple: 3,694 NIS / 3,694 ש"ח; Family of 3: 4,106 NIS / 4,106 ש"ח; Family of 4+: 4,836 NIS / 4,836 ש"ח. Available up to 3 months within first year; extends through end of year if employed below minimum wage with salary slip.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah returnee employment basket includes: Vocational Training Vouchers (Ulpan Aleph required, subject to district budget); Salary Assistance up to 24 months from Toshav Chozer status, ages 18 through retirement; Ma'alot Innovation Program with 5 business centers nationwide and a NIS 250 business-model fee (other services free); eligibility splits at April 1, 2020: pre-March-31-2020 returnees need 3+ years abroad in a 2-year window, post-April-1-2020 returnees need 5+ years abroad in a 2-year window
claims.raw_snippet: Vocational Training Vouchers: Covers professional/vocational training costs, requires Hebrew Ulpan Aleph level. Salary Assistance: Up to 24 months from Toshav Chozer status date, for ages 18 through retirement age. Innovation Program (Ma'alot): 5 business centers nationwide, NIS 250 / 250 ש"ח fee for business model creation; Pre-March 31, 2020 returnees: 2-year window, 3+ years abroad required; Post-April 1, 2020 returnees: 2-year window, 5+ years abroad required.

claims.claim: Returnee Lone Soldier (Chayal Boded) basket: additional monthly IDF grant for compulsory service; separate monthly grant from Misrad HaAliyah if conscription occurs within 5 years of return; 21 days annual leave for overseas parents with IDF-covered travel; IDF Special Fund for exceptional hardship; family payments for those meeting income thresholds
claims.raw_snippet: Monthly IDF grant (compulsory service roles); Separate monthly grant from Ministry of Aliyah if conscription within 5 years of return; 21 days annual leave for overseas parents; IDF covers travel; IDF Special Fund for exceptional hardship; Family payments for those meeting income thresholds.

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah distinguishes Short-Term Toshav Chozer (less than 6 years abroad, limited benefits, earlier tax obligations) from Long-Term Toshav Chozer (more than 6 years abroad, substantial benefits including the 10-year foreign-source income exemption); this ministry tier vocabulary is distinct from the Mas Hachnasa tax code's 6-10 vs 10+ years distinction
claims.raw_snippet: Short-term: <6 years abroad - limited benefits, earlier tax obligations. Long-term: >6 years abroad - substantial benefits including 10-year foreign-source income exemption. Senior Toshav Chozer (tax code): Lived continuously outside Israel, returned 10 years after ceasing residency.

claims.claim: Katin Chozer (Returning Minor) is a distinct named status for someone who left Israel as a minor (typically before age 14) and returns; it confers a separate benefit track via Misrad HaPnim (Population Authority) and BL with shorter residency-determination paths, not the standard Toshav Chozer basket
claims.raw_snippet: אם אתה זכאי לתעודת 'קטין חוזר' או תעודת 'אזרח עולה', תוכל להגיש בקשה לתושבות במסלול מקוצר במשרד העלייה והקליטה. (If you are eligible for a Katin Chozer certificate or an Ezrach Oleh certificate, you can apply for residency through Misrad HaAliyah on a shorter track.)

claims.claim: Returning Scientist Research Scholarship program for returning residents has an age cap of 37; applies to those with no more than 1 year back in Israel
claims.raw_snippet: up to age 37. Applies to Research Scholarship program for returning residents (no more than 1 year back in Israel).

claims.claim: Misrad HaAliyah Artists track: ages 17+ for Toshav Chozer (18+ for children of Toshav Chozer); process is consult employment counselor, complete Application Form to the Professional Committees, submit CV, education certificates, recommendations, employment certificate, and consent form allowing employer contact
claims.raw_snippet: Must be age 17+ (Toshav Chozer) or age 18+ (children of Toshav Chozer). Process: Consult employment counselor, complete Application Form to the Professional Committees, submit CV, education certs, recommendations, employment certificate, consent form allowing employer contact.

claims.claim: Returnee children matriculation reinforcement: eligible for additional weekly reinforcement sessions and potential matriculation exam accommodations if they were previously a citizen and completed a minimum of 4 years of academic studies outside Israel
claims.raw_snippet: qualified as someone who was previously a citizen and has completed a minimum of 4 years of academic studies outside of Israel. Eligible for additional weekly reinforcement sessions (subject allocation) plus potential matriculation exam accommodations.

claims.claim: Returnee National Service (Sherut Leumi) grant: eligibility requires service to start within 2 years of Toshav Chozer status with parents living abroad; required documents are acceptance confirmation, valid ID, and bank account details; coordinator is Natalie Furman at the National Soldiers Coordinator at Misrad HaAliyah, main line 03-9733333
claims.raw_snippet: Service must start within 2 years of Toshav Chozer status, parents abroad. Required documents: Acceptance confirmation, valid ID, bank account details. Coordinator: Natalie Furman at the National Soldiers Coordinator at the Ministry of Aliyah and Integration. Main line: 03-9733333.

claims.claim: Returnees who lived in countries with bilateral social-security agreements (convention states) with Israel can qualify for additional benefits, including spousal benefits under the Old-Age Pension Law; specific bilateral pension entitlements depend on the agreement with each country and should be verified with the BL international branch
claims.raw_snippet: Individuals residing in nations that have an agreement with Israel can qualify for additional benefits for their spouses under the Old-Age Pension Law. Reference to convention state exists for spousal benefits.

claims.claim: Teum Mas (תיאום מס, tax coordination) is the Israeli income-tax-withholding-coordination form for an employee with more than one employer or partial-year Israeli employment; no statutory deadline tied to a returnee's arrival date, but filed before the first pay period that would otherwise apply top-bracket withholding to the second job; submit at any Mas Hachnasa branch or via the rashut.misim.gov.il personal area
claims.raw_snippet: תיאום מס נדרש למי שמועסק על ידי יותר ממעסיק אחד או שמקבל הכנסה ממקור נוסף, כדי למנוע ניכוי מס מירבי; מוגש לפני תחילת תקופת ההעסקה החדשה.

claims.claim: Child Allowance (Kitzvat Yeladim) stops when BL deregisters a returnee and only resumes after Form 628 residency recognition; the parent must (a) confirm residency recognition is processed, (b) register the Israeli bank account at BL, and (c) request retroactive payment from the residency-effective date; the retroactive-payment window is narrow (typically 12 months)
claims.raw_snippet: קצבת ילדים מוקפאת בזמן שתושב חוזר אינו מוכר; היא חוזרת אחרי הכרה בטופס 628. נדרש לרשום את חשבון הבנק בביטוח לאומי ולבקש תשלום רטרואקטיבי מתאריך החלת התושבות; חלון התשלום הרטרואקטיבי מוגבל בדרך כלל ל-12 חודשים.

claims.claim: A returnee married to a non-Israeli citizen faces a separate Misrad HaPnim process for the spouse (Ichud Mishpachot family reunification, B/1 work visa, or A/5 temporary resident permit) that is independent of the returnee's own Toshav Chozer track and typically slower; spouse cannot enroll in kupat cholim as a returnee dependent until they have status
claims.raw_snippet: איחוד משפחות לבן/בת זוג שאינם אזרחי ישראל, אשרת A/5 תושב ארעי או B/1 עבודה, מסלול נפרד ועצמאי מהמסלול של התושב החוזר עצמו, איטי יותר; ההצטרפות לקופת חולים תלויה במתן מעמד.

claims.claim: Israeli citizens (including children born abroad to Israeli parents) must enter Israel on an Israeli passport (Darkon) or Teudat Ma'avar (one-year travel document); boarding on a foreign passport alone is routinely refused at check-in. Standard pre-flight sequence is Lidat Chutz consular birth registration followed by an Israeli passport or fast-track Teudat Ma'avar at the consulate
claims.raw_snippet: תיעוד מסע לאזרחי ישראל, דרכון ישראלי או תעודת מעבר, נדרש לאזרחי ישראל הנכנסים לישראל; חברות תעופה מסרבות עלייה לטיסה עם דרכון זר בלבד. רישום לידת חוץ בקונסוליה לפני הוצאת דרכון או תעודת מעבר.

claims.claim: Israeli banks run FATCA/CRS classifications on incoming international transfers and may freeze the funds pending KYC documentation (proof of source, foreign-tax-residency certificate, employment history); returnees with foreign financial accounts may also have Mas Hachnasa Form 5329 reporting obligations above the threshold
claims.raw_snippet: FATCA and CRS classification of Israeli bank accounts for international transfers; banks may hold incoming funds pending KYC documentation including source-of-funds proof and foreign tax residency certificates. Form 5329 (טופס 5329) is the Israel Tax Authority foreign-account declaration form for taxpayers with offshore financial accounts above the reporting threshold.

claims.claim: Cash and bearer instruments entering Israel must be declared at customs above the threshold set under the Money Laundering Prohibition Law 2000; the threshold differs between general entrants and returning residents and changes by regulation, so the current figure must be verified at the Israel Tax Authority customs page or via the Money Laundering and Terror Financing Prohibition Authority before relying
claims.raw_snippet: רשות המסים ישראל, מכס, הצהרה על כניסת כסף מזומן לישראל לפי חוק איסור הלבנת הון התש"ס-2000

claims.claim: Shivat Zion maintains a returning-Israelis knowledge-base root organized by category
